title: grants — data retention windows
description: Retention per model + per grant state. Active grants have no floor (they live until revoked or expired). Terminal grants (revoked / expired) hard-purge at 90 days for allow decisions; deny decisions retain INDEFINITELY (an inviolable block that ever existed is compliance-worthy even after revocation — someone may need to know 'this user was once explicitly denied access to this resource'). The `reason` field is retained indefinitely regardless of state — audit trail is the promise.

retention.description: Active grants have no TTL. Live until revoke or expiry.
90-day hot hold after revoke/expiry for admin visibility ('why did Bob lose access last Tuesday'). Then hard-deleted by PurgeRevokedGrantsJob / PurgeExpiredGrantsJob.
Deny grants retained INDEFINITELY. Deny was ever issued = compliance evidence — a compromised admin may have lifted a legitimate deny; the audit trail lets us reconstruct the timeline.
retention.erasureOnRequest: P0D (never — deny grants are anonymised on erasure, never deleted)

retention.description: The `reason` column value is retained INDEFINITELY per audit policy. Never redacted after grant expiry / revoke. Erasure requests anonymise the subject reference in `reason` (if any) but preserve the text itself.
activity_log rows emitted by the HasActivityLog trait are retained 1 year per compliance policy (owned by activity module — this module just states its expectation).
audit rows emitted by the HasAudit trait retain 7 years per SOC 2 evidence retention. Owned by audit module.

complianceTiers.customer_access_data: Customer-owned records (allow grants). Retained for the customer's benefit until the retention window. Subject to erasure on request.
complianceTiers.audit_log: Immutable audit records (deny grants + reason fields). Retained for the shortest of the compliance regime maximum + the operational SLA. Anonymised, not deleted, on erasure requests.

identityErasure.description: When a User's Identity is erased under GDPR Art. 17, this module cascades: every grant WHERE subject is that User is hard-deleted (allow grants) or anonymised (deny grants — subject_id → 'erased-<hash>').

tenantErasure.description: When a Tenant is erased, EVERY grant in the tenant is hard-deleted via the tenant_id CASCADE FK. Activity_log + audit rows travel with their own retention.

resourceErasure.description: When a grantable resource is hard-deleted, grants on it are auto-revoked with revoked_reason='orphaned_resource' by ReconcileOrphanedGrantsJob within the following week. For an immediate cascade, consumer modules can emit `resource::HardDeleted` events that this module listens to (deferred).
